analysis.doc: Synchronized from Linear issue identifier: POL-453

POL-453.title: IMPLEMENT: fix adopt dry-run gating + add OKF docs-only migration recipe
POL-453.body:
## Objective

Make `polaris adopt consolidate --dry-run` actually dry, and give operators a docs-only path to migrate existing smartdocs into the OKF-style structure without running the full adopt flow.

## Context

Analyzed from POL-452. `reconcileAgentFiles` (src/cli/adopt-genesis.ts) and `migrateSmartDocs` (src/cli/adopt-smartdocs.ts) both ignore `plan.dry_run`/`dryRun` entirely and perform real writes even when `--dry-run` is passed and prompts are declined. See POL-452 analysis comment for full evidence.

## Goal

- `--dry-run` produces zero working-tree diff for both smartdocs migration and agent-file reconciliation.
- Operators can migrate/reformat existing smartdocs to OKF structure without touching CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md or running the 8-phase adopt.

## Scope

* src/cli/adopt-genesis.ts
* src/cli/adopt-smartdocs.ts
* src/cli/adopt-command.ts
* src/smartdocs-engine/index.ts (docs command composition only, no new engine logic)

## Acceptance Criteria

- `polaris adopt consolidate --dry-run` on a repo with an existing CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md and pending smartdocs moves produces zero working-tree diff.
- Declining the archive prompt under `--dry-run` never calls writeFileSync/mkdirSync/renameSync.
- A documented or scripted composition of `polaris docs migrate` + `polaris docs seed-index --all` + `polaris docs seed-instructions --all` achieves OKF-only migration without invoking `reconcileAgentFiles`.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/cli/adopt-command.test.ts src/cli/adopt-smartdocs.test.ts

## Ordering

Children execute in listed order; no cross-dependency required, but the dry-run fix (child 1) should land before documenting/validating the docs-only recipe (child 2).

## Non-goals

- No changes to the interactive archive/compression feature's actual (non-dry-run) behavior.
- No new smartdocs-engine commands â€” compose existing `docs` subcommands only.

POL-454.title: Gate migrateSmartDocs and reconcileAgentFiles writes behind dry-run
POL-454.body:
## Objective

`polaris adopt consolidate --dry-run` must not write anything to the working tree.

## Context

Analyzed in POL-452. Two independent write paths ignore dry-run today:
1. `reconcileAgentFiles(repoRoot)` in src/cli/adopt-genesis.ts â€” no `dryRun` param exists on `ReconcileOptions` (currently `{ anthropicKey?: string; now?: Date }`). 4 unconditional write sites: the pointer-comment write on decline (~line 128), the genesis doctrine file write (~line 208), the thin-pointer replacement write (~line 213), and provenance write via `appendGenesisProvenance` (~line 82/225).
2. `migrateSmartDocs(plan, repoRoot)` in src/cli/adopt-smartdocs.ts â€” never reads `plan.dry_run` anywhere despite the field existing on `AdoptionPlan`. Unconditionally runs `git mv`/`renameSync`, writes `smartdocs/index.md`/`log.md` via `scaffoldBundleRoot`, and writes `.polaris/adoption-plan.json` (`savePlan`) + `.polaris/adoption-provenance.json` (`saveProvenance`).

## Goal

Add `dryRun` gating to both `reconcileAgentFiles` (extend `ReconcileOptions` with `dryRun?: boolean`) and `migrateSmartDocs` (read `plan.dry_run`), mirroring the existing correct idiom in `src/smartdocs-engine/index.ts`'s `docs migrate --dry-run` / `docs migrate-provenance --dry-run`: compute what would happen, print a `[dry-run] would ...` line per action, skip the actual write/rename/mkdir call, still return a normal result shape. In `src/cli/adopt-command.ts`, thread the dry-run flag from the `consolidate` case (lines ~202-209) into both calls â€” `AdoptPhaseOptions.dryRun` already exists as a field but is never read; either wire it here or derive dry-run consistently from `options.plan.dry_run` (pick one source of truth and use it in both call sites).

## Scope

* src/cli/adopt-genesis.ts
* src/cli/adopt-smartdocs.ts
* src/cli/adopt-command.ts

## Acceptance Criteria

- With `--dry-run`, `reconcileAgentFiles` performs zero `writeFileSync`/`mkdirSync` calls and does not launch the interactive archive prompt in a way that can write on decline â€” either skip the prompt under dry-run or ensure decline path never writes.
- With `--dry-run`, `migrateSmartDocs` performs zero `git mv`/`renameSync`/`writeFileSync`/`mkdirSync` calls, and instead logs/returns what it would do.
- Running `polaris adopt consolidate --dry-run` against a repo fixture with an existing CLAUDE.md and pending smartdocs candidates produces zero diff on the temp repo.
- Existing non-dry-run behavior (real archive, real migration) is unchanged.

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* npx vitest run src/cli/adopt-command.test.ts src/cli/adopt-smartdocs.test.ts

## Ordering

- None â€” no blocking siblings.

## Non-goals

- No changes to the Anthropic-backed compression/archival logic itself when not in dry-run mode.
- No changes to `docs migrate --dry-run`/`docs migrate-provenance --dry-run` (already correct; used as reference only).

POL-455.title: Add composed docs-only OKF migration recipe (no agent-file changes)
POL-455.body:
## Objective

Give operators a documented (or thinly scripted) way to migrate/reformat existing smartdocs into the OKF-style structure without touching agent instruction files or running the full 8-phase `polaris adopt`.

## Context

Analyzed in POL-452/POL-453. `migrateSmartDocs` (src/cli/adopt-smartdocs.ts) and the seed functions in `src/smartdocs-engine/seed-instructions.ts` (`seedIndex`, `seedIndexAll`, `seedInstructions`, `seedInstructionsAll`, `seedSummary`, `seedSummaryAll`) are already fully decoupled from `reconcileAgentFiles`. A CLI command family already exists (`src/smartdocs-engine/index.ts`, registered via `createDocsCommand()` in src/cli/index.ts): `polaris docs migrate [--dry-run]`, `polaris docs seed-index [path] [--all]`, `polaris docs seed-instructions [path] [--all]`, plus `seed-summary`, `audit`, `validate-instructions`.

This means the "docs-only OKF migration" ask does not require new engine code â€” it can be satisfied by documenting the correct composition of existing commands (and optionally wrapping them in one convenience command if that's clearly better UX).

## Goal

An operator who already has smartdocs content but wants it reformatted to OKF structure (index.md + `okf_version` frontmatter per src/smartdocs-engine/seed-instructions.ts) can do so in one documented step, without invoking `polaris adopt` or `reconcileAgentFiles`.

## Scope

* src/smartdocs-engine/index.ts (only if a thin composed command is added â€” no new migration/seeding logic)
* docs/ or README (recipe documentation)

## Acceptance Criteria

- Running `polaris docs migrate` + `polaris docs seed-index --all` + `polaris docs seed-instructions --all` in sequence (or a single new composed command that just calls these three) migrates and OKF-formats existing smartdocs.
- No CLAUDE.md/AGENTS.md/agent instruction file is touched by this recipe.
- The recipe/command is documented somewhere discoverable (CLI `--help` text and/or docs/).

## Validation

* npm run build
* npm test
* Manual: run the recipe against a repo with pre-existing non-OKF smartdocs/ content and confirm index.md files gain `okf_version` frontmatter and no agent files change.

## Ordering

- Depends on POL-454 landing first only in the sense that dry-run trustworthiness should be verified before this recipe is documented as safe to preview; not a hard blocker for implementation.

## Non-goals

- No new smartdocs-engine ingestion/classification logic.
- No changes to the full `polaris adopt` 8-phase flow.
